clinical_criteria: SBP > 140 mmHg or DBP > 90 mmHg on at least two outpatient measurements at least one month apart
≥4 simultaneous antihypertensive medication classes on at least 2 occasions (1 month apart) for Case Type 1
3 simultaneous antihypertensive medication classes on at least 2 occasions (1 month apart) for Case Type 2
GFR < 30 ml/min (exclusion criterion using MDRD formula)
For controls: all SBP < 135 and DBP < 90 one month AFTER BP medications
Medications must have dose, strength, route, or frequency present with medication name for NLP validation
algorithm_summary: This algorithm identifies resistant hypertension cases through two types: Type 1 requires ≥4 simultaneous antihypertensive medication classes on ≥2 occasions (1 month apart); Type 2 requires 3 simultaneous medication classes plus elevated BP measurements (SBP >140 or DBP >90) at least one month after meeting medication criteria. The algorithm excludes patients with specific conditions (malignant adrenal tumors, secondary hypertension, heart disease, kidney disease, congenital anomalies), temporarily excludes those with thyroid disorders or sleep apnea within 5 years, and excludes those with severe kidney dysfunction (GFR <30) or heart failure (EF <35%). Two control groups are defined: controlled hypertension (patients on single BP medication with controlled BP) and no hypertension (no elevated BP measurements or antihypertensive medications). The algorithm specifies detailed medication classes including ACE inhibitors, ARBs, diuretics, beta blockers, calcium channel blockers, and others, with combination medications counting as multiple classes.
